title: Workflow DAG Definition
description: Contract 3 (orchestration extension) — the STATIC workflow graph an orchestrator agent executes: a set of nodes (subtasks) and directed dependency edges. DEFINED NOW for alignment; ENFORCEMENT TURNS ON in Phase 10 (Orchestrator). The graph MUST be acyclic (validated via Kahn's algorithm before execution; a cycle yields error_code=INVALID_DAG and no nodes are spawned). Internal orchestrator->sub-agent execution runs each node in-tenant on behalf of the assigned sub-agent (no A2A); A2A delegation is reserved for the external/cross-vendor boundary. The `subtask_dag` JSONB column on xagent.workflows serializes a document of this shape.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this contract schema, e.g. "1.0.0".

tenant_id.description: Organisation/tenant UUID that owns this workflow.

name.description: Human-readable workflow name (e.g. the template name, or a slug of the goal).

version.description: Optional semantic version of the workflow definition itself (for reusable templates).

goal.description: The natural-language goal this DAG decomposes.

decomposition.description: How this DAG was produced: a deterministic template match, or an LLM decomposition (the plan task-type).

nodes.description: The subtask nodes of the DAG.

edges.description: Directed dependency edges. An edge from A to B means B depends on A (A must complete first).

constraints.description: Execution caps that bound cost and runaway fan-out.

max_depth.description: Maximum delegation depth. Default aligns with MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH (5).

max_fanout.description: Maximum concurrent/total sub-agents fanned out from a single node or run.

max_cost_usd.description: Per-workflow cost ceiling in USD; on breach the run is cancelled (early stop).

max_tokens.description: Per-workflow token ceiling; on breach the run is cancelled (early stop).

hil_gates.description: Nodes that require a human-in-the-loop approval before execution (Auth HIL / Contract 16).

node.description: One subtask node in the DAG.

node_id.description: Unique node identifier within this DAG (referenced by edges).

node_type.description: Node kind. `agent`/`task` run a sub-agent pipeline; `tool`/`skill` invoke a capability; `approval`/`human` pause for HIL; `condition`/`fanout`/`join` are control nodes.

ref.description: What this node runs: a task_type (a2a/task-types.md), a `<server>.<tool>` id, a skill id, or a model alias — interpreted per node_type.

assigned_agent_id.description: The sub-agent this node runs as (must be a sub-agent of the orchestrator, same tenant). Resolved from `preset` when omitted.

preset.description: Name of an agent preset (xagent.agent_presets) bundling {system prompt, tool allowlist, scope subset, model tier} for this node.

input_bindings.description: Template bindings for this node's input, e.g. {"context": "{{node-a.output}}"}; resolved from upstream node outputs at execution time.

retry.description: Bounded retry policy for this node.

on_error.description: What to do when this node fails: `fail` the workflow, `skip` the node (partial result), or `continue` downstream.

edge.description: A directed dependency edge (from must complete before to).

condition.description: Optional guard expression evaluated against the from_node output (conditional edges).

hil_gate.description: A human-in-the-loop gate on a node.

operation_type.description: HIL operation type matched against the orchestrator's ask_on_triggers (known values: tool_execution, sub_agent_creation, llm_restriction, skill_execution). Free string for forward-compatibility.

approval_scopes.description: Scopes a Contract-16 step-up token must grant to approve this gate (when step-up is enforced).

approval_resource.description: Resource the approval binds to, e.g. workflow:<workflow_id>.

$comment: additionalProperties:true throughout for forward-compatibility — this is an orchestration envelope later phases extend. Enforcement is Phase 10; services MUST accept this shape now and MUST NOT enforce it before then. Cycle detection (Kahn) is mandatory before execution; a cycle yields error_code=INVALID_DAG. Sibling: workflows/run.schema.json (the runtime instance / subtask tree).
